answer:
Based on the Hono atlas (specifically ADR-02 "Router Interface and Presets"), here is the contract a custom RegExpRouter replacement must satisfy.

## The `Router<T>` interface (`src/router.ts`)

```ts
export interface Router<T> {
  name: string
  add(method: string, path: string, handler: T): void
  match(method: string, path: string): Result<T>
}
```

That's the entire shape, but several hard constraints hang off it.

## Hard constraints (from ADR-02)

1. **Implement the full interface — no partials.** Partial implementations break preset swappability. (`Router`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, etc. all conform.)

2. **Return the dual-shape `Result<T>`** — *both* representations are part of the public contract:
   ```ts
   type Result<T> =
     | [[T, ParamIndexMap][], ParamStash]   // compact form
     | [[T, Params][]]                       // expanded form
   ```
   Consumers downstream (`compose()`, `Context` construction, `app.routes` introspection) already handle both, so pick whichever fits your implementation — just be consistent and emit a valid shape.

3. **Throw `UnsupportedPathError`** for any path shape you cannot encode. This is how `SmartRouter` (the default preset's outer router) catches failure and falls through to the next router. If you plan to be used inside SmartRouter, throwing is mandatory; swallowing silently will break fallback.

4. **Support the full path grammar accepted by *any* existing router.** If you're replacing `RegExpRouter` as a drop-in in the `hono` preset (`SmartRouter[RegExpRouter, TrieRouter]`), your accepted grammar must be at least as broad as RegExpRouter's — otherwise a preset swap silently changes which routes work.

5. **`Router<T>` is a public API.** Users can (and do) pass `new Hono({ router: yourRouter })` directly, so anything observable — including `name` — is part of the contract.

## Soft/contextual things to know

- **`name` is read by introspection/tests.** SmartRouter is allowed to mutate its own name on first match (e.g. `'SmartRouter' → 'SmartRouter + RegExpRouter'`). Your `name` should be a stable identifier; pick one and don't mutate it unless you're imitating SmartRouter's hot-swap trick.
- **No runtime config flags — presets are separate module graphs.** If you want your router shipped as a preset (`hono/yours`), that's a new entry point, not a feature toggle. Removing an existing preset (`hono/quick`, `hono/tiny`) is a breaking change.
- **Benchmarks are expected.** ADR-02 treats "removing routers or presets without benchmark evidence" as unsupported; the same bar applies to introducing one seriously — there's existing scaffolding under `benchmarks/routers-deno/` (see `regExpRouter`, `trieRouter`, `patternRouter` entries) you can plug into.

## Reference implementations to crib from

All of these are already in the repo and satisfy the contract — read them in roughly this order of complexity:

- `src/router/pattern-router/router.ts` — simplest, backed by platform `URLPattern`
- `src/router/linear-router/router.ts` — naive linear scan
- `src/router/trie-router/router.ts` — trie, falls back after RegExp
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts` — what you're replacing
- `src/router/smart-router/router.ts` — wraps others, demonstrates the `UnsupportedPathError` fallback dance

## Minimum checklist before you swap

- [ ] `name: string` set to a stable value
- [ ] `add(method, path, handler): void` accepts all method strings Hono emits (including `ALL`)
- [ ] `match(method, path): Result<T>` returns one of the two `Result<T>` shapes
- [ ] Unsupported paths throw `UnsupportedPathError` (imported from `src/router.ts`)
- [ ] Path grammar covers everything `RegExpRouter` accepts (params, wildcards, optional segments, regex constraints)
- [ ] Benchmarks in `benchmarks/routers-deno/` show the perf story you expect

If you want to bypass presets entirely and just use it in your own app, `new Hono({ router: new YourRouter() })` is the documented escape hatch.

args.command: ls atlases/hono/ && file atlases/hono/index.db && sqlite3 atlases/hono/index.db ".schema" 2>&1 | head -60
